primary.note: All 21 parts fully captured; 17-21 fetched on 2026-07-17 via the Chrome extension (URL hash #part-N/M + get_page_text).

datedPosts.note: Dated posts. Fetch once, then only track status. Rank: official docs > these > fan-made. `discoveredVia` records how the URL was established, not what it says.

entries.discoveredVia: href read from the engineering index
href read from the engineering index
href read from the engineering index
href read from the engineering index (featured, no date shown)
href read from the engineering index
href read from the engineering index
search result (title + URL, repeated); date from the result

living.note: Re-check each run. Change detection uses a stable `changeMarker`, never a content hash: WebFetch returns processed text that is not byte-stable, so a hash would fire on every run (same reason the primary source stores no raw hashes).

entries.note: URL not yet confirmed — the repo is search-confirmed, this raw path is inferred. The first fetch must confirm it; on 404 record `incomplete` and report.
Fetch the raw README (raw.githubusercontent.com/anthropics/skills/main/README.md) rather than the HTML page.

secondaryFanMade.note: Grows chapter by chapter, like the primary source. Mark anything taken from it as "(fan-made, Willison)"; never adopt a safety-/API-relevant claim without doc verification.
secondaryFanMade.discoveredVia: search result (title + URL + announcement; "first two chapters")

contextOnly.note: Framing and orientation only — NOT part of the verification canon. Never adopt a claim from these without independent doc verification. Cite by source name when used.

entries.note: PDF — WebFetch often cannot read these. On failure record `incomplete` and fetch in the main run via the browser tool (see MAINTENANCE, known environment pitfalls).

openItems: Fan-made source fully captured (parts 1-21). Next run: only check for new parts.

2026 sources declared but NOT yet ingested: 7 datedPosts, 5 living, 1 secondaryFanMade, 2 contextOnly — all `status: pending`. The source model is in place; the first ingestion run still has to happen.
The claude-code CHANGELOG raw URL is `urlVerified: false` — the first fetch must confirm or correct it.

confirmed: dontAsk is a valid permission mode (previously mismarked as invalid).
fable is an official alias = Claude Fable 5 (not speculative).
Effort low/medium/high/xhigh persist, max session-only; effortLevel setting only up to xhigh.
diverged: --tmux on the worktree does not exist (belongs to Agent Teams, not worktrees).
inherit is not a model alias, only a subagent-model value.

note: tabs = observed tab count per part; the next run compares title/count to detect new/changed content. No raw-content hashes stored (the source only partially delivers content via WebFetch).
